degree_class: LM-29 - Class of Master's Degrees in Electronic Engineering

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent with a minimum three-year undergraduate background in related subjects, together with proven skills in Electronics, Informatics and/or Information Engineering, and Physics and Mathematics. Country-based entry-title and GPA requirements also apply.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor's degree or equivalent; minimum three-year undergraduate degree in related subjects.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must show proven skills in Electronics, Informatics and/or Information Engineering, and Physics and Mathematics.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements apply on the official application platform. No universal programme-specific GPA threshold is separately published on the course page.

accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The third call is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Applicants may apply to the programme only once per academic year.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The third call is restricted to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Applicants may apply to the programme only once per academic year.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: EU or equivalent applicants may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy.
Upload is required at application stage.
The general checklist asks for the academic qualification if it has already been awarded.
Listed in the official general application checklist.
Listed in the official general application checklist.
Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Other specific documents required for the chosen degree programme
required_documents.notes: The platform checklist may request programme-specific additional items after the degree programme is added to the application.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalized
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalized
required_documents.notes: The transcript should be issued by the competent academic authority.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and D visa for study-enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly by 15 July and then apply for a study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants who are in Italy at enrolment stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Entry test on basic analog/digital electronics, mathematics and physics
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed public 2026/2027 test dates were found on the reviewed official programme factsheet [uncertain].
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The official programme factsheet states that an entry test is required, but it does not publish the scoring method or passing threshold on the reviewed page [uncertain].

source_quotes.quote: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) is required in related subjects
Entry test on basic analog/digital electronics, mathematics and physics
31st August for open access degree programmes.
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)

uncertainty_notes: The reviewed 2026/2027 official programme factsheet states that an entry test is required, but it does not publish the test calendar or scoring threshold on that page.
